description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly gray surface interlaced by striking white mineral veins. The rock's texture appears coarse and rugged, with visible grain and uneven surfaces, suggesting it is composed mainly of granite or a similar hard stone. The white streaks run across multiple faces, creating a visually interesting contrast against the darker gray matrix. Some areas of the rock are more heavily covered in white mineral deposits, while others show mostly the gray stone. The overall form is rounded but not smooth, with some flat and some bulging surfaces. This type of rock could serve as a natural barrier or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A gray rock with bold white mineral veins and a rough, irregular surface, featuring both rounded and flat areas.
description_view_0: This view highlights the elongated side of the rock, showing prominent white veins crossing the gray stone diagonally.
description_view_1: This angle reveals a large patch of white mineral on one end, with less visible veining and more solid gray surface.
description_view_2: The side here is dominated by white mineral coverage, with only a few gray patches visible, giving it a lighter appearance.
description_view_3: This view shows a mix of gray and white, with the white mineral forming irregular patches and the gray stone more exposed.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
